qa_pairs.question: Why is it important for users to select appropriate parameter values when submitting jobs on computing clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Choosing appropriate parameter values helps ensure that your job doesn't waste resources or cause issues for other users and yourself. It also leads to quicker job starts and a higher likelihood of finishing correctly, ultimately producing the output needed for your research.

qa_pairs.question: What are some typical problems users might encounter if they don't choose appropriate resource requests for their jobs on a cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Common problems include longer wait times in the queue if too many resources (time, memory, CPU cores, GPUs) are requested. Conversely, jobs can be stopped if insufficient resources are requested and they exceed their time or memory limits. Additionally, estimating resources based on local computer performance can be misleading due to differing processor types and speeds, and compute tasks might waste resources if the program doesn't scale well, isn't designed for multiple nodes, or processors are idle due to read-write operations.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended approach for new users to estimate the time and memory needed for their jobs on a computing cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The best approach is to start by submitting a few relatively small test jobs, requesting standard amounts of memory (e.g., `#SBATCH --mem-per-cpu=2G`) and time (e.g., one or two hours). If a job finishes prematurely, double the duration; if it ends with an 'OOM event' (out of memory), double the requested memory. These test jobs help users gain familiarity to make intelligent estimates for more realistic jobs.
